request_payload.ai_prompt_hint: Treat this as a checklist: list triggered flags first, then explain each using flag_value vs threshold, severity, flag_description, and recommended_action. Keep non-triggered flags brief.
request_payload.actionable_question: What specific risk signals are active for me right now?

risk_flags.flag_description: Average score is below the pass threshold for this dataset scale.
risk_flags.recommended_action: Review the weakest assessment topics and schedule tutor support before the next assessment.

risk_flags.flag_description: This student has previous attempts, which can indicate accumulated difficulty or re-enrolment risk.
risk_flags.recommended_action: Check prior attempt context and confirm whether the student needs a catch-up plan.

risk_flags.flag_description: Engagement score is above the safety threshold.
risk_flags.recommended_action: Maintain a consistent weekly study routine and keep using course resources.

risk_flags.flag_description: Submission punctuality is below the expected threshold.
risk_flags.recommended_action: Use deadline reminders and submit drafts earlier to reduce late-submission risk.

risk_flags.flag_description: Scores are trending downward across assessments.
risk_flags.recommended_action: Compare recent feedback with earlier stronger work and intervene before the next assessment.

confidence.reason: 1998 students × 9 assessments across 8 weeks — strong statistical basis.

explanation.summary: You have a few important risk signals that indicate areas where you can improve. These include repeated attempts, low punctuality, and a negative trend in your scores. Addressing these can help you get back on track.

insights.title: Repeated Attempts
insights.description: You have 1 previous attempt, which suggests you may be facing some challenges. This can indicate accumulated difficulty or a risk of re-enrollment.

insights.title: Low Punctuality
insights.description: Your submission punctuality is currently at 0, which is below the expected threshold of 0.7. This can lead to late submissions and affect your overall performance.

evidence.context: This indicates a need for better time management.

insights.title: Negative Trend in Scores
insights.description: Your scores are trending downward with a value of -0.7188, which is concerning. This suggests that you may need to reassess your study strategies.

evidence.context: This indicates a decline in performance.

educational_implications: It's important to address these areas to improve your academic performance and overall engagement.

recommendations.action: Check prior attempt context and confirm whether you need a catch-up plan.
recommendations.rationale: Understanding your previous attempts can help identify specific areas where you may need additional support.

recommendations.action: Use deadline reminders and submit drafts earlier to reduce late-submission risk.
recommendations.rationale: Improving your punctuality can help you manage your workload better and avoid penalties.

recommendations.action: Compare recent feedback with earlier stronger work and intervene before the next assessment.
recommendations.rationale: This can help you identify what has changed and how to improve your scores moving forward.

confidence.reason: The data is clear and indicates specific areas of concern.

task_output_contract: Use checklist style: triggered flags first, then non-triggered flags briefly.
For triggered flags, state flag_value versus threshold plus severity, description, support category, and existing recommended_action when supplied.
Do not invent new risk flags or new recommendations outside returned flag evidence.

raw_text:
Summary: You have a few important risk signals that indicate areas where you can improve. These include repeated attempts, low punctuality, and a negative trend in your scores. Addressing these can help you get back on track.

Insights: Repeated Attempts: You have 1 previous attempt, which suggests you may be facing some challenges. This can indicate accumulated difficulty or a risk of re-enrollment. | Low Punctuality: Your submission punctuality is currently at 0, which is below the expected threshold of 0.7. This can lead to late submissions and affect your overall performance. | Negative Trend in Scores: Your scores are trending downward with a value of -0.7188, which is concerning. This suggests that you may need to reassess your study strategies.

Educational implications: It's important to address these areas to improve your academic performance and overall engagement.

Recommendations: high - Check prior attempt context and confirm whether you need a catch-up plan. - Understanding your previous attempts can help identify specific areas where you may need additional support. | high - Use deadline reminders and submit drafts earlier to reduce late-submission risk. - Improving your punctuality can help you manage your workload better and avoid penalties. | high - Compare recent feedback with earlier stronger work and intervene before the next assessment. - This can help you identify what has changed and how to improve your scores moving forward.
